plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable income and broad market exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration within the portfolio.
This strategy aims for capital preservation, reducing overall portfolio volatility and leverage.
Maintain a significant cash buffer to absorb potential market downturns.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets a stable ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expected swings are much smaller, but so is potential upside.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits and manage overall exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL, as both are below their stops and are leveraged, reducing overall portfolio risk.
Reduce SMH exposure to 10% given its current downtrend status.
Buy AMD at 5% as a strong, high-scoring long setup from the scanner.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan balances growth potential with risk management, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding a high-quality setup. Expect moderate swings and a more controlled risk profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate, freeing capital for higher-conviction plays.
Sell SOXL and SPAL as they are below their stops, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Reduce SMH to 10% due to its downtrend, focusing on uptrending assets.
Buy AMD at 10% and SNOW at 5%, both high-scoring long setups from the scanner.
This strategy leans into high-conviction setups and maintains some tech concentration.
Stops will be strictly enforced to manage the amplified risk of this approach.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth. It concentrates in strong setups and uses leverage, but this amplifies both upside and drawdown. Leveraged ETFs decay daily; expect significant swings and potential for rapid losses if stops are not strictly honored.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reallocate for income.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged exposure, aligning with income goals.
Sell SMH to reduce high-beta sector exposure and focus on stability.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single name concentration.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize monthly distribution potential.
This plan prioritizes steady distributions and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income approach targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta exposure for stability. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in selloffs; this is not a savings account but aims for cash-like income with lower drawdown.
